title: rom-librarian user.json
description: User-specific deployment configuration for rom-librarian. Created on first run, updated by the skill after each session.

created.description: ISO 8601 date this file was first created

last_updated.description: ISO 8601 date this file was last modified

deployment.description: Describes the user's hardware and software environment

frontend.description: Frontend ID — must match a key in static.json frontends (e.g. retrobat, batocera, es-de, launchbox)

frontend_version.description: Installed frontend version string, e.g. '2.1.0'. Null if unknown.

os.description: Operating system where the primary frontend is actually running. This is the path interpretation perspective for frontend-managed metadata and playlists.

agent_os.description: Operating system where rom-librarian commands are executed. This can differ from deployment.os when running under WSL or SSH.

wsl.description: True if scripts and Claude Code run under Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL). Affects path handling for Windows-native tools like 7-Zip.

rom_root.description: Absolute path to the ROM root directory. Use Linux/WSL path when wsl=true (e.g. /mnt/g/games/emulation/roms).

bios_root.description: Absolute path to the BIOS directory, or null if using frontend default

preferences.description: User/deployment policy choices that guide diagnosis and repair planning.

faux_m3u_dirs.description: Global policy for directories named <game>.m3u/. ask = report and confirm before conversion; forbid = convert to real playlist files when planning repairs; allow = acceptable if supported; preserve = do not convert without explicit targeted approval.

faux_m3u_dirs_by_system.description: Per-system override for faux_m3u_dirs. Keys should match system IDs from static.json.

playlist_path_separator.description: Global preference for generated playlist path separators. auto/target_os means use the active frontend or emulator expectation rather than the agent OS.

playlist_path_separator_by_system.description: Per-system playlist separator override for unusual frontend/emulator expectations.

systems.description: Per-system status and path overrides. Keys are system IDs matching static.json systems.

enabled.description: Whether this system is actively managed by rom-librarian

status.description: untouched = not yet inspected, partial = in-progress or known issues remain, clean = fully organized and verified

rom_path.description: Absolute path to where this system's ROMs live (overrides static.json default if set)

images_path.description: Absolute path to where disc images live (may differ from rom_path to avoid ES double-detection)

favorites_path.description: Absolute path to the subfolder the frontend reads (e.g. a Favorite/ subfolder within the system root)

multidisc_override.description: Override the multidisc handling method for this system (e.g. m3u_in_rom_root)

frontend_os.description: Per-system override for the OS perspective used by frontend metadata, if it differs from deployment.os.

emulator.description: Primary emulator/core used for this system when known. Use this to capture emulator-specific ROM format or playlist expectations.

emulator_os.description: OS perspective of the actual emulator process when it differs from the frontend, such as a Windows frontend launching a Linux/WSL tool or vice versa.

playlist_path_separator.description: Per-system separator expectation for generated .m3u entries. Use null/auto unless a frontend or emulator has a known unusual expectation.

notes.description: Free-text notes about this system's state, quirks encountered, or special handling

last_worked.description: ISO 8601 date when this system was last worked on

overrides.description: Sparse overrides to static.json data. user.json wins on any conflict.

frontends.description: Override fields on specific frontends. Same structure as static.json frontends but only include fields that differ.

systems.description: Override fields on specific systems. Same structure as static.json systems but only include fields that differ.

learned.description: Deployment-specific discoveries appended by the skill after each session

system.description: System ID this applies to, or null if applies broadly

frontend.description: Frontend ID this applies to, or null if applies broadly

category.description: quirk = unexpected behavior, path = path discovery, structure = filesystem layout, format = file format detail
